hero_product_name: the AnyWell powered wheelchair from the white-background reference photos
campaign_goal: Create emotionally restrained vertical brand-film test assets for AnyWell-inspired mobility advertising for Western markets without referencing any real identifiable customer.
target_market: United States, Canada, United Kingdom, and Western Europe
target_audience: Obese or large-bodied Western older adults, their spouses, adult children ages 35-55, and families evaluating thoughtful mobility support for everyday outdoor life.
core_selling_points:
- smooth dual-drive confidence
- steady outdoor support
- gentle starts and controlled turns
- comfort that helps more moments happen outside
use_scenarios:
- backyard path
- home ramp and porch exit
- quiet evening neighborhood route
visual_style: Warm, cinematic, realistic, portrait-first, restrained, mature North American and Western European brand advertising.
style_tone: warm, credible, calm, cinematic, never melodramatic
consistency_anchor: Match the real AnyWell wheelchair shown in the white-background product photos, but present it only in its normal open riding position. Preserve the front/side riding silhouette, right-side joystick, armrests, supportive seat cushion scale, side housing, front caster, large drive wheel profile, tubular frame, footrest shape, and paired upper rear push handles visible as slim handles above or just behind the backrest from side/front-profile angles. Do not make the backrest, rear panel, rear red fabric detail, or lower rear area a hero feature. Do not show any rear/lower external battery box, dangling battery cable, removable battery pack, folded chair, semi-folded chair, folding demonstration, compact storage form, rear-facing view, or rear three-quarter product angle.
additional_info: All scenes must feel newly generated and anonymous for Western audiences, but the wheelchair itself should match the real product from the white-background reference images as closely as possible. Cast the rider as a dignified obese or large-bodied Western older adult with natural posture, realistic proportions, and stable identity across scenes; do not slim the rider down, age-swap, or make body size comedic, pitiful, or exaggerated. Do not reproduce any real customer, quote, house, unfinished ramp build, or woods footage. Hard product exclusions: no visible rear/lower battery pack or exposed battery cable, and no folded, half-folded, collapsed, storage, or folding/unfolding view of the wheelchair. The paired upper rear push handles must remain visible as slim handles above or just behind the backrest in side/front-profile shots; do not crop them out, omit them, or let them dissolve into the backrest. The back panel and lower rear quadrant of the chair must stay hidden, cropped out, or fully occluded in every shot, while the upper push handles remain readable. Never render a rectangular box behind or below the seat. Never place the camera behind the rider; every scene must show the rider's front torso or soft facial profile, plus his right forearm and right hand on the right-side joystick during self-operated motion. Operation logic: whenever the wheelchair moves under rider control, the rider's right hand must visibly rest on or gently hold the right-side joystick controller; if the hand is not on the joystick, an adult must visibly push from the handles, with both hands and both push handles clearly readable. For this campaign, prefer self-operated right-hand-on-joystick motion and do not show the partner pushing. White-background product photos are identity references only and must never appear as white studio frames, packshots, cutaways, or flash frames in the storyboard or video.
music: Warm restrained cinematic underscore with light piano and gentle ambient texture.

concepts.title: Nature Within Reach
concepts.core_theme: Freedom returns through quiet outdoor exploration.
concepts.main_theme: Nature does not have to stay out of reach.
concepts.hook: Nature does not have to feel far away.
concepts.cta: Discover more thoughtful outdoor confidence with AnyWell.
concepts.visual_style: Morning woodland edge, soft movement, natural realism, faces never sharply hero-framed.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the same anonymous large-bodied silver-haired older man and the same partner across all scenes. Preserve his heavier senior body type, wardrobe, posture, and soft non-identifiable face consistently.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- wooded backyard edge
- narrow garden path
- quiet overlook near trees
concepts.additional_info: The partner follows with affection, not as a caregiver stereotype. The tone is calm and dignified. In every shot, the paired upper rear push handles should remain visible as subtle slim handles above or just behind the backrest without revealing the lower rear battery area.

shots.scene_description: A large-bodied silver-haired older man in a clean knit jacket sits in an anonymous powered wheelchair at the back doorway of a quiet American home. His right hand rests visibly on the right-side joystick controller, ready to self-operate. The camera is in front of the rider on the joystick side, so the viewer sees his chest, soft facial profile, right forearm, joystick hand, front caster, armrest, and side profile of the chair. His partner appears softly off to the side, relaxed and smiling, never touching the chair. Faces stay natural and not sharply featured. The paired upper rear push handles are visible as slim handles above or just behind the backrest, while the rider's back and the chair's back panel are not visible, the lower rear quadrant is outside the frame, and no external battery pack or cable appears. No white-background product-photo frame appears.
shots.camera_movement: slow handheld-style dolly from the front/joystick side of the doorway into daylight, steady and calm
shots.lighting: soft morning natural light with gentle window spill and cool outdoor highlights
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait front-side composition, rider centered low in frame with chest and joystick hand visible, doorway and trees creating depth, no readable signage
shots.transition_anchor: end with the wheelchair rolling forward toward the outdoor path so the next shot can continue the same direction
shots.voiceover_en: Some paths feel closer the moment confidence returns.
shots.subtitle_zh: 当安心回来，自然也不再遥远。
shots.sfx: soft tire roll, birds in the distance

shots.scene_description: The same large-bodied older man glides along a narrow wooded backyard path in the same anonymous powered wheelchair, self-operating with his right hand visibly on the right-side joystick controller. The partner walks parallel a few steps to the side without touching the chair. The camera tracks ahead of him from a joystick-side front-profile angle, so the viewer sees his front torso or soft facial profile, right forearm, joystick hand, front caster, armrest, side wheel profile, and paired upper rear push handles as slim handles above or just behind the backrest. The frame feels cinematic and realistic, not glossy or synthetic. The rider's back and the chair's back panel are never shown. The wheelchair stays fully open for riding; the lower rear quadrant remains hidden or cropped out, with no rear lower battery shape. No white studio product-photo insert appears.
shots.camera_movement: smooth joystick-side front-profile tracking shot with subtle push-in as the chair settles into the route
shots.lighting: filtered daylight through trees with realistic contrast and soft shadows
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: mid portrait joystick-side front-profile framing, rider chest and joystick hand readable, path and trees wrapping around the chair, partner lightly out of focus to the side
shots.transition_anchor: finish with forward motion continuing toward a brighter opening in the trees
shots.voiceover_en: A steady ride can make the outdoors feel welcoming again.
shots.subtitle_zh: 稳定前行，让户外重新变得亲近。
shots.key_message: Outdoor confidence can feel gentle and steady.
shots.sfx: soft gravel texture, leaves moving lightly

shots.scene_description: The route widens slightly. The same partner walks beside the wheelchair now, matching pace and glancing over with quiet warmth. The large-bodied man keeps moving independently, right hand visibly resting on the right-side joystick controller while the chair moves. The camera moves backward in front of them from the joystick side, showing the rider's chest or soft facial profile, right forearm, joystick hand, front/side chair profile, and paired upper rear push handles as slim handles above or just behind the backrest, never the rider's back or the chair's rear panel. The shot is intimate but restrained, prioritizing dignity, companionship, and motion through nature rather than dramatic emotion. No white-background product-photo frame appears.
shots.camera_movement: front-side tracking shot moving backward smoothly as both subjects advance together
shots.lighting: neutral daylight with soft skin tone rolloff and realistic depth
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium-wide front-side composition, both figures visible, rider chest and joystick hand readable, trees and path framing the pair, anonymous home landscape
shots.transition_anchor: end with both looking ahead toward open space
shots.voiceover_en: Not to hold him back. Just to share the way forward.
shots.subtitle_zh: 不是拦住他，而是陪他继续向前。
shots.key_message: Companionship should support freedom, not replace it.
shots.sfx: light footsteps beside the chair, distant birds

shots.scene_description: The same couple pauses near a quiet clearing at the edge of the trees. The wheelchair comes to a gentle stop after self-operated movement, with the large-bodied rider's right hand still near the right-side joystick. Both look outward, framed from a soft front-side profile so no one becomes personally identifiable, and the viewer can still read the rider's front torso, right forearm, joystick hand, and paired upper rear push handles as slim handles above or just behind the backrest. The image holds on stillness, fresh air, and the feeling that dignity belongs outdoors too. The rider's back is not shown, and the chair's back panel and lower rear quadrant are hidden by angle, rider body, and foreground grass. No white-background product-photo frame appears.
shots.camera_movement: gentle front-side settling move into a wide portrait hero frame, then hold
shots.lighting: clean daylight with soft rim light on shoulders and wheelchair edges
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: wide portrait front-side composition, wheelchair in the foreground with joystick hand visible, trees opening to sky, elegant negative space above
shots.transition_anchor: end on a quiet still frame suitable for logo or end card
shots.subtitle_zh: 超越移动，我们守护尊严。
shots.key_message: Dignity belongs in the world outside.
shots.sfx: gentle breeze and soft outdoor ambience

concepts.title: A Gentle Start
concepts.core_theme: Home should still open toward the outdoors.
concepts.main_theme: Because dignity should never stay indoors.
concepts.cta: See how AnyWell can support more confident everyday starts.
concepts.visual_style: Back porch, wood ramp, grass edge, calm daytime realism, product motion emphasized through comfort and control.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the same anonymous large-bodied older adult and the same partner across all scenes. Preserve the rider's heavier senior body type, calm wardrobe palette, posture, and one home exterior.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- front porch threshold
- wood ramp
- grass edge beside the home
concepts.additional_info: This concept should make smooth starts and stable control feel emotional instead of technical. In every shot, the paired upper rear push handles should remain visible as subtle slim handles above or just behind the backrest without revealing the lower rear battery area.

shots.scene_description: A large-bodied silver-haired older adult in a soft blue overshirt rests in an anonymous powered wheelchair near the open front door of a modest American home. The rider's right hand is visibly on the right-side joystick controller before the chair starts moving. The camera frames the porch, wood railing, joystick side, front caster, paired upper rear push handles, and daylight beyond from a front-side angle. A partner stands near the doorway with relaxed body language, never touching the chair or rear handles. No one looks directly into camera. The feeling is readiness, not struggle. The back panel and lower rear quadrant are not visible, while the upper push handles remain readable as slim handles above or just behind the backrest. No white studio product-photo frame appears.
shots.camera_movement: slow front-side push from the doorway toward the chair as the rider prepares to move
shots.lighting: bright midday natural light with warm wooden reflections from the porch
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait composition with porch lines leading outward, wheelchair foregrounded, home details clean and generic
shots.transition_anchor: end as the chair begins a smooth forward roll toward the ramp
shots.voiceover_en: The first few feet outside should feel calm, not complicated.
shots.subtitle_zh: 迈出家门的第一程，应该安心，而不是复杂。
shots.sfx: soft porch ambience, subtle wheel start

shots.scene_description: The same large-bodied rider moves down a finished wooden ramp in the same anonymous powered wheelchair, self-operating with the right hand visibly on the right-side joystick controller. The camera stays low at the front-side corner, highlighting smooth starts, steady tracking, joystick-side control, front caster motion, paired upper rear push handles, and balanced posture without turning the shot into a product demo close-up. The home remains generic and unidentifiable. The back panel and lower rear quadrant stay out of frame or hidden by the ramp rail, while the upper push handles remain visible as slim handles above or just behind the backrest. No white-background product-photo insert appears.
shots.camera_movement: low front-side track matching the ramp descent with smooth stabilized motion
shots.lighting: clear daylight with mild highlights on the ramp and chair contours
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait frame emphasizing the ramp line, chair stability, and realistic outdoor textures
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair reaching level ground and continuing forward naturally
shots.voiceover_en: Smooth power helps every start feel more natural.
shots.subtitle_zh: 顺滑动力，让每一次出发都更自然。
shots.sfx: quiet wheel roll over wood, subtle outdoor air

shots.scene_description: At the base of the ramp, the same large-bodied rider makes a measured turn toward the grass edge, right hand visibly guiding the right-side joystick controller, while the partner walks nearby, hands free and never touching the chair. The motion is stable and unhurried. The camera captures the emotional safety of the moment rather than technical specs from a front-quarter arc. Faces remain soft and not crisply identifiable. Keep the chair in normal riding position; the paired upper rear push handles remain visible as slim handles above or just behind the backrest, while the back panel and lower rear quadrant remain hidden, with no rear lower battery or folding mechanism visible. No white-background product-photo frame appears.
shots.camera_movement: smooth arc around the front quarter of the chair as it turns toward the yard
shots.lighting: neutral daylight with believable contrast and soft shadow under the chair
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: mid portrait front-quarter framing, ramp side rail partly occluding the rear lower area, grass and open yard ahead, partner nearby but not assisting physically
shots.transition_anchor: finish with the chair facing the open yard and ready for the last glide
shots.voiceover_en: Steady support can turn a doorway into a real beginning.
shots.subtitle_zh: 稳稳支撑，让门口真正变成新的开始。
shots.key_message: Outdoor access should feel possible and composed.

shots.scene_description: The same large-bodied rider glides along the edge of the yard with the right hand visibly on the right-side joystick controller, then slows into a relaxed stop while the partner walks beside him. The environment is bright, simple, suburban, and anonymous. The ending holds on control, comfort, and the emotional idea of getting back to life outdoors. The shot stays front-side, keeps the paired upper rear push handles readable as slim handles above or just behind the backrest, and never reveals the chair's back panel or lower rear quadrant. No white-background product-photo frame appears.
shots.camera_movement: gentle front-side three-quarter hero move that settles into a still frame
shots.lighting: clean afternoon light with warm natural skin and fabric tones
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait hero frame with grass, porch, and open sky balanced around the chair
shots.transition_anchor: end on a stable hold for brand end card placement
shots.voiceover_en: Because dignity should never stay indoors.
shots.subtitle_zh: 因为尊严，不该被留在室内。
shots.key_message: Life outside should remain within reach.

concepts.title: Sunset Still Belongs to Us
concepts.core_theme: Companionship is about sharing the view again.
concepts.main_theme: There is still time to go see the light together.
concepts.hook: Some evenings are too important to miss.
concepts.cta: Explore more dignified everyday moments with AnyWell.
concepts.visual_style: Golden hour neighborhood path, soft lens bloom, intimate but restrained framing.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the same anonymous large-bodied older rider and partner across all scenes. Preserve the rider's heavier senior body type, their clothing palette, and the evening setting while keeping facial detail soft and non-identifiable.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- evening driveway exit
- neighborhood path
- sunset overlook
concepts.additional_info: This concept is about quality of life, companionship, and dignity, not speed or performance drama. In every shot, the paired upper rear push handles should remain visible as subtle slim handles above or just behind the backrest without revealing the lower rear battery area.

shots.scene_description: At sunset, a large-bodied older man in an earth-tone jacket sits in the same anonymous powered wheelchair just outside a quiet home. His right hand rests visibly on the right-side joystick controller as he prepares to move under his own control. The partner steps out beside him. Warm light falls across the porch and driveway. The shot stays respectful, cinematic, and anonymous, with no readable address or unique house details. The camera sees the chair from the front-side/joystick side only, keeping the paired upper rear push handles visible as slim handles above or just behind the backrest while the back panel and lower rear quadrant stay hidden. No white studio product-photo frame appears.
shots.camera_movement: slow drifting front-side push with the couple angled toward the open evening path
shots.lighting: golden-hour sunlight with soft bloom and long natural shadows
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait composition, warm porch tones, driveway leading outward, chair clearly readable but not over-lit
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair moving into the evening light toward the neighborhood path
shots.voiceover_en: Some evenings are too important to miss.
shots.subtitle_zh: 有些傍晚，值得再次走出去。

shots.scene_description: The same couple moves along a quiet neighborhood path lined with trees and soft evening sky. The large-bodied rider leads in the anonymous powered wheelchair, right hand visibly on the right-side joystick controller, while the partner walks beside him, matching pace and never touching the chair. The shot emphasizes quiet companionship, stable motion, and the emotional ease of being outside together again. The angle stays joystick-side front-profile, keeping the paired upper rear push handles visible as slim handles above or just behind the backrest while hiding the back panel and lower rear quadrant. No white-background product-photo insert appears.
shots.camera_movement: smooth joystick-side front-profile track with gentle parallax from trees and evening background
shots.lighting: warm low-angle sunset light with realistic contrast and soft edge highlights
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium-wide front-profile frame, path lines flowing diagonally, couple centered in motion, no sharp facial close-up
shots.transition_anchor: finish with both figures moving toward a brighter horizon line
shots.voiceover_en: A steady ride makes room for quieter, better moments.
shots.subtitle_zh: 稳定前行，才能把时间留给更好的陪伴。
shots.key_message: Stable motion creates space for shared moments.

shots.scene_description: The same large-bodied rider slows near a small overlook where the sky opens wider, right hand visibly resting on or easing the right-side joystick controller. The partner steps close, both looking outward instead of at camera. The wheelchair remains the same anonymous model in normal riding position and the scene focuses on breath, light, and togetherness, not spectacle. The shot stays front-side/profile; the paired upper rear push handles remain readable above or just behind the backrest, while the back panel and lower rear quadrant are hidden by angle, rider body, and foreground shadow. No white-background product-photo frame appears.
shots.camera_movement: slow front-side orbit into a clean side-profile composition as the chair comes to rest
shots.lighting: rich amber sunset with soft backlight and gentle atmosphere
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait frame with open sky above, chair and partner in lower third, distant trees silhouetted
shots.transition_anchor: hold on the pause so the final line can land over stillness
shots.voiceover_en: Sometimes the real luxury is simply being there together.
shots.subtitle_zh: 真正珍贵的，是还能一起站在风景里。
shots.key_message: Presence together can be the real promise.

shots.scene_description: The final frame stays with the same couple and wheelchair in a front-side silhouette against a warm sunset sky. The large-bodied rider's right hand remains near the right-side joystick, consistent with self-operated movement. They remain anonymous, calm, and still. The shot is brand-like, elegant, and emotionally mature, designed for a soft AnyWell end card without implying a testimonial or real family story. The silhouette should read as a normal open riding wheelchair with paired upper rear push handles visible above or just behind the backrest, not a folded chair; the back panel and lower rear quadrant are hidden and no rear lower battery shape is visible. No white studio product-photo frame appears.
shots.camera_movement: gentle settle into a locked front-side portrait silhouette frame
shots.lighting: sunset silhouette with warm sky gradient and subtle rim on the chair outline
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait hero frame, ample negative space for an end card, couple and chair as a quiet silhouette
shots.transition_anchor: final hold for logo, tagline, and CTA
shots.subtitle_zh: 超越移动，我们守护尊严。
shots.key_message: The promise is dignity, freedom, and shared time.
